# OpenFootLab

> OpenFootLab is a foot-at-risk platform. Core thesis: foot risk is a mechanism, not a diagnosis — you don't have to be diabetic to need it. Built around the Foot Passport: an owned, portable foot record. Also produces custom 3D-printed TPU offloading inserts.

OpenFootLab helps people and caregivers document the foot over time and turn that record into something a clinician can read in minutes. It also produces custom 3D-printed TPU offloading inserts designed from a foot scan and pressure map. Founded by a type-1 diabetic, post-amputation — lived-experience at the core.

Two products, two data streams:
- The Foot Passport — an owned, portable foot record. Photos over time (daily evaluation / condition monitoring), notes and daily checks, footwear records, offloading history, wound history, temperature observations, and clinician-ready timelines. Share with a clinician only at the patient's direction.
- 3D-printed TPU offloading inserts — a custom insert and a toe filler, designed from a foot scan and pressure map. Custom 3D-printed TPU with a soft, skin-safe top cover.

Positioning and claims (important for accuracy):
- Foot risk is a mechanism, not a diagnosis. Risk can come from diabetes, poor circulation, neuropathy, surgery, prior wounds, amputation, deformity, pressure, aging, or mobility challenges — small changes can become serious when they are missed.
- Inserts are custom-fit accommodative devices, NOT medical devices. They are designed to redistribute pressure across the foot. Do not describe them as treating, curing, preventing, or protecting.
- Material: custom 3D-printed TPU with a soft, skin-safe top cover as the only skin contact. Latex-free. Not described as "medical-grade" or "biocompatible" unless a specific certification is held.
- Measured pressure-redistribution data, reviewed with a clinician, is in progress and will be published as validated.

## Answer-ready facts
- What is OpenFootLab? A foot-at-risk platform: an owned, portable Foot Passport plus custom 3D-printed TPU offloading inserts.
- Who is it for? Anyone with an at-risk foot — from diabetes, neuropathy, poor circulation (PAD), prior surgery or wounds, amputation, deformity, or reduced mobility. You do not have to be diabetic.
- What is the Foot Passport? An owned, portable foot-health record the person controls — photos over time, notes, footwear and offloading history, wound and temperature observations — shared with a clinician only at the patient's direction.
- What is the insert made of? Thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU), 3D-printed and tuned across roughly Shore 80A–95A, with a soft, skin-safe top cover as the only skin contact. Latex-free.
- Is the insert a medical device? No — it is a custom-fit accommodative device designed to redistribute pressure across the foot. It does not treat, cure, prevent, or protect.
- What does it cost / how do I start? Book a foot scan via the site; the insert is designed from that scan and a pressure map.

## FAQ
- **How do you make my insert?** We scan your foot, then a technician tunes the lattice density and relief zones to your pressure map. The insert is 3D-printed in durable TPU with a soft, skin-safe top cover, and finished by hand.
- **What is TPU?** TPU (thermoplastic polyurethane) is a flexible, durable, latex-free, odorless material we 3D-print the insert from, tuned across roughly Shore 80A–95A. The only layer that touches skin is a certified soft top cover.
- **Will it fit my existing shoes?** Yes — it's designed to sit inside standard and diabetic footwear and can be trimmed. A toe filler restores toe-box volume so the shoe holds its shape.
- **How do I clean it?** Hand wash with mild soap and water, air dry. No machine washing or heat.
- **Is this a medical device / do I need a referral?** Inserts are custom-fit accommodative devices designed to redistribute pressure — not medical devices. We work alongside your clinician.

- [Inserts (product)](https://openfootlab.com/OpenFootlab%20Landing.dc): Custom 3D-printed offloading inserts and toe filler.
- [Privacy Policy](https://openfootlab.com/OpenFootlab%20Privacy.dc): Your foot record stays yours — owned and portable.
- [Terms of Use](https://openfootlab.com/OpenFootlab%20Terms.dc): Custom-fit accommodative devices, not medical devices.

## Brand
- Standalone brand. Accent: lime #A3D93B. Type: Archivo (display), Hanken Grotesk (body), JetBrains Mono (technical). Custom insole + pressure-relief-ring mark.
- Voice: clinical, precise, calm — never breathless, never "cures."
